# FMEA — Steering Control Functional Chain
## Road Grader Front Axle Assembly — Logical Architecture
**Generated by:** SE Knowledge Partner
**Chain UUID:** a0be678c-afea-41f8-874c-ee4a8d272109
**Date:** 2026-06-16
**Source:** LA Steering Control functional chain fabric
**Traceability:** Requirements SC-001 through SC-019

---

## Scope

This FMEA covers all functional exchanges in the LA Steering Control functional chain. Each exchange represents a potential failure point between a source function and a target function. Severity, Occurrence, and Detection are scored 1–10. RPN = S × O × D.

**End effect reference:** Loss of commanded wheel steering angle at Receive L/R Steering Angle (terminal functions of the chain).

---

| # | Item (Component) | Function | Functional Exchange | Failure Mode | Failure Effect — Local | Failure Effect — End | S | O | D | RPN | Recommended Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Hydraulic System | Supply Pressure to LH Cyl | Hydraulic Pressure to LH Cylinder | Loss of hydraulic pressure to LH cylinder | Actuate steering cylinder LH receives no fluid — no rod displacement | No LH wheel steering actuation. RH cylinder alone cannot maintain Ackermann geometry. Degraded or lost steering control | 9 | 3 | 4 | 108 | Add pressure sensor on LH supply line. Specify minimum supply pressure in requirements (SC-001). Evaluate dual-supply redundancy at LA |
| 2 | Hydraulic System | Supply Pressure to LH Cyl | Hydraulic Pressure to LH Cylinder | Degraded hydraulic pressure (below 14 MPa) | LH cylinder produces insufficient rod force — partial actuation | Reduced LH wheel deflection. Left/right steering asymmetry. Possible instability on left turns | 7 | 4 | 5 | 140 | Add pressure monitoring requirement. SC-001 lower bound (14 MPa) must be enforced by system relief valve. Add periodic pressure verification to maintenance schedule |
| 3 | Hydraulic System | Supply Pressure to RH Cyl | Hydraulic Pressure to RH Cylinder | Loss of hydraulic pressure to RH cylinder | Actuate steering cylinder RH receives no fluid — no rod displacement | No RH wheel steering actuation. LH cylinder alone drives primary knuckle but RH knuckle loses synchronized force | 9 | 3 | 4 | 108 | Mirror of item 1. Monitor RH supply independently. Consider single-valve dual-outlet design to ensure both cylinders receive supply simultaneously |
| 4 | Hydraulic System | Supply Pressure to RH Cyl | Hydraulic Pressure to RH Cylinder | Degraded hydraulic pressure (below 14 MPa) | RH cylinder produces insufficient rod force | Reduced RH knuckle rotation. Ackermann synchronization degraded. Tire scrub on right turns | 7 | 4 | 5 | 140 | SC-001 applies to both supply lines. Require balanced supply pressure across LH and RH circuits |
| 5 | Steering Cylinder LH | Actuate steering cylinder LH | Steering LH | Cylinder rod seizure / mechanical binding | No linear displacement output to LH knuckle | LH knuckle cannot rotate. Total loss of LH wheel steering. Vehicle cannot turn left | 10 | 2 | 5 | 100 | Specify maximum cylinder friction in requirements. Add cylinder seal inspection to maintenance schedule (SC-018). Consider position sensor for rod detection |
| 6 | Steering Cylinder LH | Actuate steering cylinder LH | Steering LH | Cylinder rod slow / partial stroke | Reduced displacement output — LH knuckle under-rotates | LH wheel steering angle below commanded value. Left/right asymmetry. SC-008 ±0.5° sync tolerance potentially exceeded | 7 | 3 | 6 | 126 | Add stroke position sensor. Require minimum stroke rate at rated flow (SC-005 — 4.0 s lock-to-lock) |
| 7 | Steering Cylinder RH | Actuate steering cylinder RH | Steering RH | Cylinder rod seizure / mechanical binding | No linear displacement output to RH knuckle | RH knuckle cannot rotate. Tie rod receives no synchronization input from RH side. LH knuckle driven independently | 10 | 2 | 5 | 100 | Same as item 5. RH cylinder seizure is partially mitigated by LH cylinder continuing to drive primary knuckle, but sync is lost |
| 8 | Steering Cylinder RH | Actuate steering cylinder RH | Steering RH | Cylinder leakage past piston seal | Slow pressure loss — rod force degrades over time | Progressive degradation of RH actuation force. SC-019 leakage limit (1 mL/hr) exceedance | 6 | 4 | 4 | 96 | Seal inspection interval per SC-018. Add leakage detection requirement. Specify maximum permissible internal leakage rate |
| 9 | Steering Knuckle RH | Rotate RH Knuckle | Cylinder Displacement | Knuckle binds on kingpin — no rotation | No displacement signal to Synchronize wheel steering angles | Tie rod receives no input. LH knuckle synchronization reference lost. Both wheels may lock | 10 | 2 | 4 | 80 | Kingpin lubrication schedule per SC-018. Specify maximum kingpin friction torque. Add kingpin bushing wear limit to maintenance criteria |
| 10 | Steering Knuckle RH | Rotate RH Knuckle | RH Knuckle Rotation Output | Knuckle rotation angle less than commanded | Reduced rotation delivered to Deliver R Rotation | RH wheel under-deflected. Left/right wheel angle mismatch exceeds SC-008 ±0.5° tolerance | 7 | 3 | 5 | 105 | Kingpin geometry inspection. Tie rod length adjustment procedure. SC-008 Ackermann tolerance requirement applies |
| 11 | Tie Rod Assembly | Synchronize wheel steering angles | Sync Angle to LH Knuckle | Tie rod fracture | LH knuckle receives no synchronization correction | LH wheel continues at cylinder-commanded angle without Ackermann correction. Tire scrub. Progressive wheel divergence on sustained turns | 9 | 2 | 4 | 72 | Tie rod endurance per SC-010 (500k cycles). Specify minimum tie rod tensile strength. Add visible inspection criterion for tie rod damage |
| 12 | Tie Rod Assembly | Synchronize wheel steering angles | Sync Angle to LH Knuckle | Tie rod elongation / deformation | Reduced synchronization correction accuracy | Ackermann geometry degraded. SC-008 ±0.5° sync tolerance exceeded under load | 7 | 3 | 4 | 84 | Specify maximum tie rod deflection (SC-009 — 2.0 mm midpoint). Add deflection check to maintenance schedule |
| 13 | Tie Rod Assembly | Synchronize wheel steering angles | Synchronized Wheel Angle | Loss of synchronization output to Limit steering travel | Limit steering travel receives no angle reference | Steering stop engagement reference lost. Mechanical stops alone must prevent over-travel — no functional limiting | 8 | 2 | 5 | 80 | Verify mechanical stops as primary travel limit (SC-011). Functional chain limit is secondary — mechanical stop must be sufficient standalone |
| 14 | Steering Stop Assembly | Limit steering travel | Constrain L Steering Angle | Steering stop fails to engage LH | LH knuckle receives no travel constraint | LH wheel over-travels beyond 36°. Wheel-to-chassis contact risk. SC-011 maximum angle exceeded | 9 | 2 | 3 | 54 | SC-012 — stops must withstand 72 kN stall force. Inspect stop engagement at service intervals. Add over-travel detection requirement |
| 15 | Steering Stop Assembly | Limit steering travel | Constrain R Steering Angle | Steering stop fails to engage RH | RH knuckle receives no travel constraint | RH wheel over-travels beyond 36°. SC-011 maximum angle exceeded | 9 | 2 | 3 | 54 | Same as item 14. Both stops must be independently verified |
| 16 | Steering Stop Assembly | Limit steering travel | Constrain L Steering Angle | Stop engagement angle drifts outside ±1° tolerance | LH knuckle constrained at wrong angle | LH wheel maximum angle incorrect. Left/right lock symmetry exceeds 1.0° tolerance per SC-011 | 6 | 3 | 4 | 72 | Stop angle inspection and adjustment procedure. Specify stop angle setting tolerance and verification method |
| 17 | Steering Knuckle LH | Rotate LH Knuckle | LH Knuckle Rotation Output | LH knuckle binds on kingpin | No rotation output to Deliver L Rotation | LH wheel cannot be deflected. Complete loss of LH wheel steering | 10 | 2 | 4 | 80 | Same as item 9 (RH knuckle). Kingpin lubrication and bushing inspection |
| 18 | Left Wheel Hub | Deliver L Rotation | LH Steering Angle Delivered | Wheel hub fails to deliver angle to wheel | Receive L Steering Angle receives no input | LH wheel does not respond to commanded angle despite knuckle rotating correctly | 8 | 2 | 5 | 80 | Verify wheel hub-to-knuckle attachment integrity. Specify minimum torque transfer at wheel hub (SC-014 — 2,800 N·m) |
| 19 | Right Wheel Hub | Deliver R Rotation | RH Steering Angle Delivered | Wheel hub fails to deliver angle to wheel | Receive R Steering Angle receives no input | RH wheel does not respond to commanded angle | 8 | 2 | 5 | 80 | Same as item 18. SC-014 applies to both hubs |
| 20 | Left Wheel (actor) | Receive L Steering Angle | — | Wheel/tyre fails to maintain contact at commanded angle | Commanded angle not transferred to road surface | Vehicle heading not corrected despite axle assembly functioning correctly | 7 | 2 | 6 | 84 | Outside scope of axle assembly. Flag to vehicle-level FMEA. Tyre specification and inflation requirements |

## Top 5 Highest RPN Items

| Rank | # | Item | Failure Mode | RPN | Priority Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2 | Hydraulic System — LH | Degraded pressure below 14 MPa | **140** | Pressure monitoring + SC-001 enforcement |
| 2 | 4 | Hydraulic System — RH | Degraded pressure below 14 MPa | **140** | Balanced supply pressure requirement |
| 3 | 6 | Steering Cylinder LH | Cylinder slow / partial stroke | **126** | Stroke position sensor + SC-005 rate requirement |
| 4 | 1 | Hydraulic System — LH | Loss of pressure | **108** | Pressure sensor on LH supply line |
| 5 | 3 | Hydraulic System — RH | Loss of pressure | **108** | Independent RH supply monitoring |

---

## Gaps Identified

**1. No property values on cylinder components**
Steering Cylinder LH and RH have no mass, bore, or pressure rating property values. Severity scores for cylinder failure modes (items 5–8) are based on engineering judgment. Adding bore diameter and rated pressure would allow quantitative force calculations to refine S scores.

**2. Hydraulic System actor has no allocated functions with property values**
Supply pressure range is captured in SC-001 (14–21 MPa) but not as a model property value on the Hydraulic System component. Adding a `Supply Pressure` property value group would make items 1–4 directly traceable to model data.

**3. Wheel hub components have no torque property values**
SC-014 specifies 2,800 N·m minimum hub torque but no property value exists on Left/Right Wheel Hub components. Items 18–19 severity scores would be better grounded with this data.

**4. Receive L/R Steering Angle functions have no allocated physical components**
The terminal functions of the chain (Receive L/R Steering Angle) are parented under `Receive steering angle output` but have no PA component allocation. Items 20–21 are therefore actor-boundary failures and fall outside the axle assembly scope — flagged for vehicle-level FMEA.

---

## Recommended Next Steps

1. **Add property values** to Steering Cylinder LH/RH (bore, stroke, rated pressure) and Wheel Hub LH/RH (rated torque) to ground S scores quantitatively
2. **Add supply pressure property value** to Hydraulic System actor
3. **Add SC-001, SC-005, SC-008, SC-011, SC-014** requirement traces to corresponding FMEA items via model requirements in SA layer
4. **Create FMEA for Camber Control chain** — several items (tie rod, knuckle) appear in both chains and the combined RPN should inform design priority
5. **Review items 5 and 7** (cylinder seizure, RPN 100) — both are single points of failure with no redundancy at the component level. The dual-cylinder design provides partial mitigation but LH and RH cylinder failures have different end effects — worth independent safety assessment
